strands.title: CYCLE 51: SMOKE TEST COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY — LOG_READER VALIDATION PASSED, FULL PROJECTION READY TO EXECUTE
strands.content: Cycle 50 initiated smoke test strategy (dependency sync → parsing validation → full execution). Cycle 51 receives completion of STEP 1 and STEP 2. **STEP 1 RESULT (uv sync, turn 151)**: uv dependency resolution SUCCEEDED. Virtual environment created in .venv. 17 packages resolved and installed in <1 second. Dependency state is HEALTHY: hamutay==0.1.0 installed from source, pydantic==2.12.5 (core dependency), anthropic==0.84.0 (API client), typing-extensions==4.15.0 (modern type hints). Python 3.14.3 confirmed as runtime. **No errors, no warnings**. Dependencies are current and compatible. This **resolves Cycle 50 open question**: 'Did uv sync command complete successfully without errors?' → **YES, confirmed**. **STEP 2 RESULT (parsing validation test, turn 152-153)**: Executed `uv run python -c` to directly test log_reader.read_session_jsonl() import and execution. **Result: SUCCESS**. Found 55 turns in sample session (d5da3c7a-...). First 6 turns sampled and printed: turns 1-2 are user/assistant role exchanges (reconstructed/continued turns with content previews visible), turns 3-4 are infrastructure (tool_use), turns 5-6 are tool_result. Parsing is working correctly: (1) read_session_jsonl() imported without syntax errors, (2) Path() object passed correctly, (3) Function returned list of ConversationTurn objects, (4) Each turn has turn_number, role, content fields populated, (5) Content is correctly extracted from nested message structure (not showing raw JSON). **This resolves Cycle 50 open questions**: 'Do all required imports resolve?' → **YES**. 'Can read_session_jsonl() be imported and called?' → **YES**. 'Does it return valid ConversationTurn objects?' → **YES**. **SMOKE TEST GATE CLEARED**. All preconditions for full projection execution are satisfied. Dependencies installed, code executable, parsing validated on real data. **Next action**: Execute full run_projection with 55-turn sample session and 50-turn limit.

key_claims.text: uv sync completed successfully without errors; 17 packages installed and verified
log_reader.py update (from Cycle 49) is syntactically correct and importable
read_session_jsonl() successfully parsed real session JSONL file and returned 55 valid ConversationTurn objects
Content extraction from nested message structure (_extract_text) is working correctly; infrastructure entries (tool_use, tool_result) are included in turn stream
All Cycle 50 smoke test preconditions are satisfied; full projection execution can proceed without further dependency or parsing validation

strands.title: EMPIRICAL DATA POINT ADDED — SESSION CONTAINS 55 TURNS; PARSING OBSERVATION REVEALS MIXED TURN TYPES IN SEQUENCE
strands.content: Cycle 49 obtained sample session path. Cycle 50 validated that parsing works. Cycle 51 **adds empirical fact**: sample session d5da3c7a-3c3c-4047-a4ee-276be57567c1.jsonl contains exactly **55 conversation turns**. This is a NEW FACT (not predicted, now OBSERVED). Run_projection was configured with max_turns=50, meaning projector will process turns 1-50 and halt at turn 50 boundary. **Turn Type Composition**: Parsing output shows turn sequence structure: (1) [turn 1] user turn (initial prompt, 'Build a replay module...'), (2) [turn 2] assistant turn (thinking/reasoning), (3-4) [turns 3-4] assistant turns with [tool_use: Read] (infrastructure), (5-6) [turns 5-6] user turns with [tool_result] (infrastructure). This is **mixed turn sequence**: genuine conversational turns (user/assistant) interleaved with infrastructure turns (tool_use, tool_result metadata). **Implications for Projector**: Projector will receive turns 1-50 from this 55-turn session. It will encounter BOTH semantic turns (the conversation) AND infrastructure turns (Claude's tool calls and their results). Cycle 49 assumed this would happen; Cycle 51 confirms it is actually happening. Projector architecture must handle mixed turn types OR schema was designed to strip infrastructure. **Need to verify**: Are turns 3-4 (tool_use) and turns 5-6 (tool_result) semantic entities (included in tensor projection) or are they filtered out by ConversationTurn logic? Parsing test shows they ARE in the turn list returned by read_session_jsonl (they appear in 'turns[:6]' printout), suggesting they ARE included in projection stream.

key_claims.text: Sample session d5da3c7a-... contains exactly 55 turns (empirical observation, not estimate)
Run_projection will process turns 1-50 of this 55-turn session (50-turn limit means first 50 turns projected, last 5 ignored)
Turn sequence includes mixed types: user/assistant semantic turns AND tool_use/tool_result infrastructure turns
read_session_jsonl() returns infrastructure turns (tool_use, tool_result) as part of turn stream; they are NOT filtered out
Projector will receive turn stream containing both semantic dialogue and infrastructure metadata; unknown if projector treats these differently

strands.title: EXECUTION GATE FULLY OPEN — MOVING FROM VALIDATION TO PRODUCTION: FULL PROJECTION RUN AUTHORIZED
strands.content: Cycle 50 conditional gate was: 'Execute full projection IF smoke test passes.' Cycle 51 confirms smoke test PASSED. Gate condition satisfied. **Gate Status Progression**: Cycle 48 opened conditional execution gate pending schema validation (✓ satisfied Cycle 49). Cycle 49 satisfied schema validation pending code update (✓ satisfied by log_reader fix). Cycle 50 initiated smoke test pending dependency/syntax validation (✓ satisfied Cycle 51). Cycle 51 gate is now FULLY OPEN: no remaining conditional blockers. **Authorization to Execute**: Full projection run_projection with sample session d5da3c7a-... is AUTHORIZED and READY. No further validation steps required. **Confidence Assessment Update**: Cycle 50 estimated readiness at 0.93-0.95 (conditional on smoke test). Cycle 51 can now increase confidence to 0.96+: (1) Dependencies proven working, (2) Code proven importable, (3) Parsing proven functional on real data, (4) Session data confirmed exists and is valid. Only remaining unknowns are: (A) Does Projector logic execute without runtime errors? (B) Do tensor outputs have coherent content? These are questions answerable only by RUNNING projection, not by validation. Running projection is now SAFE and AUTHORIZED; cost is low (API tokens for 50 turns of processing). **Next immediate action**: Execute `python -m hamutay.run_projection <session_file> <output_dir> <max_turns>` with sample session. Output will be tensor_cycle_*.json files in output_tensors/.

key_claims.text: All conditional preconditions for full projection execution are satisfied; gate is FULLY OPEN
Smoke test passed all three steps: uv sync succeeded, imports resolved, parsing validated on real data
Confidence in readiness increased from 0.93 (conditional) to 0.96+ (validated); only runtime behavior remains unknown
Full projection execution is safe, authorized, and ready to proceed; cost is moderate (50 turns of API processing)
Remaining unknowns (Projector logic correctness, tensor output quality) are answerable only by execution, not validation

strands.title: CYCLE 50 OPEN QUESTIONS — MANY NOW RESOLVED; REMAINING QUESTIONS SHIFT TO EXECUTION AND OUTPUT INSPECTION
strands.content: Cycle 50 identified 13 open questions. Cycle 51 resolves or reprioritizes them. **RESOLVED by Smoke Test**: Q1: 'Did uv sync command complete successfully?' → **YES, confirmed turn 151**. Q2: 'What Python packages are now available?' → **17 packages listed, versions confirmed current (pydantic 2.12.5, anthropic 0.84.0, typing-extensions 4.15.0)**. Q3: 'Do all required imports resolve?' → **YES, confirmed turn 152 (read_session_jsonl imported, executed)**. Q4: 'Can read_session_jsonl() be imported and called?' → **YES, confirmed turn 153 (returned 55 ConversationTurn objects)**. **PARTIALLY RESOLVED**: Q10: 'Is there a way to quick-check if run_projection.py command-line interface is correct?' → Not tested directly, but uv run succeeded which suggests imports and basic structure are correct. Still unknown: exact arg ordering, error handling if args wrong. **DEFERRED TO POST-EXECUTION**: Q5: 'What is the correct parsing validation test: read sample session, mock data, or both?' → Cycle 51 used read real session (optimal choice). Q6: 'After smoke test, when should full run_projection be executed?' → **ANSWER: Immediately after parsing validates. Cycle 51 is prepared to initiate turn 154**. Q7: 'How many session files exist in baseline_run1/session/?' → Can be checked post-execution if needed; still deferred. Q8: 'If uv sync reveals dependency issues, what is recovery plan?' → Not needed (uv sync succeeded). Q9: 'Should parsing validation output actual tensor cycles or just verify ConversationTurn objects?' → Cycle 51 verified ConversationTurn objects only; full projection will output tensor cycles. Q11: 'After full execution, what should be first inspection target?' → Cycle 52 will check: file count, JSON validity, tensor content coherence (in that order). Q12: 'Python version compatibility?' → Confirmed 3.14.3; no issues observed. Q13: 'If parsing validation succeeds but full projection fails mid-way, where should debugging focus?' → Will know post-execution; prepared to debug Projector logic, memory, or timeout based on error.

key_claims.text: Smoke test resolved 4 critical open questions directly; 2 more partially resolved
Remaining open questions are appropriately deferrable to post-execution phase; none block immediate projection execution
Full projection execution should proceed immediately (turn 154+); no further validation steps required
Next cycle (Cycle 52) will shift from validation questions to output inspection questions (file count, content coherence, quality metrics)

strands.title: CYCLE 50 DECLARED LOSSES — REVIEWING WHAT WAS NOT RECOVERED, WHAT NEW LOSSES EMERGE IN CYCLE 51
strands.content: Cycle 50 declared 6 losses; Cycle 51 reviews which remain unresolved and identifies new losses. **Loss 1 (Cycle 50): Semantic content of sample user turn not analyzed** → Status: REMAINS DEFERRED. Cycle 51 did not analyze 'Build a replay module...' content. Will defer until full conversation context available post-execution. **Loss 2 (Cycle 50): Directory listing of baseline_run1/session/** → Status: REMAINS DEFERRED. Cycle 51 focused on single session; did not enumerate other files. Can be checked post-execution if needed for multi-session strategy. **Loss 3 (Cycle 50): log_reader.py syntax verification** → Status: **RECOVERED**. Cycle 51 executed log_reader.py successfully; no syntax errors found. This loss is TRANSFORMED into empirical success: import succeeded, parsing succeeded. **Loss 4 (Cycle 50): Unclear if session JSONL is correct data source** → Status: REMAINS DEFERRED. Cycle 51 confirmed session exists and parses correctly, but coherence/fitness cannot be evaluated until tensor outputs reviewed. **Loss 5 (Cycle 50): Tensor quality evaluation criteria not defined** → Status: REMAINS DEFERRED. Cycle 51 will not define criteria until first outputs available. **Loss 6 (Cycle 50): User turn content implications not analyzed** → Status: REMAINS DEFERRED. No analysis of 'Build a replay module...' or implications for projection. **NEW LOSSES IN CYCLE 51**: (1) Parsing test sampled only first 6 turns; did not verify that turns 50-55 are also correctly parsed (tail of session is unexamined). (2) Parsing test did not validate turn_number field correctness; assumed turn_number increments properly but didn't verify. (3) Parsing test showed turn types (user/assistant/tool_use/tool_result) but didn't verify these are correctly extracted from message structure. (4) Unknown if run_projection.py expects specific output_tensors directory path format or if any path works. (5) Unknown if 50-turn limit is enforced as 'project first 50 turns' or 'project until 50 tensor cycles' — are turns and cycles 1:1 or does projector emit multiple cycles per turn?

key_claims.text: Cycle 50 Loss 3 (log_reader syntax) is RECOVERED; code executed successfully with no errors
Other Cycle 50 losses remain unresolved and appropriately deferred to post-execution phase
Cycle 51 introduces 5 new losses: tail-of-session parsing unverified, turn_number correctness unverified, turn type extraction unverified, output path format unknown, turn-to-cycle mapping unclear
New losses are DECLARED explicitly; they will be addressed in post-execution inspection (Cycle 52+) or during full projection debugging

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Tail-of-session parsing validation — Parsing test sampled turns 1-6 only; did not verify that turns 50-55 are parsed correctly
declared_losses.why: Parsing validation test ran `turns[:6]` to show first 6 turns. This is quick validation, not exhaustive. Tail of session (turns 50-55) could have different structure or parsing errors not caught by head sample. Trade-off: speed vs. completeness.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Turn numbering correctness — Parsing test did not verify that turn_number field increments correctly (1, 2, 3, ..., 55)
declared_losses.why: Test printed turns but didn't check turn_number continuity or correctness. Assumed turn_number is properly assigned. If turn_number is wrong, projector might skip turns or double-count. Unknown without explicit validation.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Turn type extraction validation — Parsing test shows turn types (user/assistant/tool_use/tool_result) but didn't verify these are correctly extracted from nested message structure
declared_losses.why: Test printed roles (user/assistant) and inferred types from content preview (tool_use/tool_result), but didn't verify the extraction logic against raw message.role field. Cycle 49 fix assumes role extraction works; Cycle 51 did not independently verify.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Output directory path specification — Unknown if run_projection expects specific output path format or if relative/absolute paths both work
declared_losses.why: Command will use './output_tensors' (relative path). Unknown if run_projection creates this directory, expects pre-existing directory, or fails if path format is wrong. Will be discovered when execution runs.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Turn-to-tensor-cycle mapping semantics — Unknown if 50-turn limit means 'process 50 turns' or 'emit 50 tensor cycles'; unclear if turn and cycle are 1:1 or if projector emits multiple cycles per turn
declared_losses.why: Instructions refer to 'max_turns=50' but unknown if this maps to 50 tensor_cycle files or if projector logic creates multiple cycles per turn. Parsing test returned 55 turns; full execution with max_turns=50 will clarify this mapping.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Full log of uv sync output details — Cycle 51 shows summary (17 packages installed) but doesn't retain detailed version numbers or build logs
declared_losses.why: Tool result truncated to key info; full build logs not stored. If dependency issue emerges later, detailed build log would be helpful for debugging. Trade-off: verbosity vs. conciseness.

open_questions: Will full projection execution (turn 154+) complete without runtime errors?
How many tensor_cycle_*.json files will be created? Will count match 50 (max_turns) or will it be different?
Are tensor_cycle JSON files valid and non-empty? Do they contain coherent semantic content?
What does tensor output structure look like? What fields are in each tensor_cycle JSON?
Does projector handle mixed turn types (user/assistant/tool_use/tool_result) correctly, or does it expect only semantic turns?
Is output_tensors/ directory created automatically by run_projection, or must it exist beforehand?
What is the wall-clock runtime for 50-turn projection? Seconds, minutes, or longer?
If full projection fails, at which turn does it fail? Is there error logging or does it crash silently?
Are all 50 turns projected equally, or do later turns show degraded/simplified tensor content due to context window limits?
How does Projector handle the 50-turn limit? Does it truncate input at turn 50, or does it process all 55 turns and output only first 50 cycles?
After full execution, how should tensor outputs be evaluated? What metrics indicate success vs. failure?
Should multi-session processing begin immediately after first session succeeds, or should first session be fully analyzed before scaling?
Are there any preliminary insights from the 55-turn session structure that inform what to expect from projector outputs?
